questions.question: Sales for $120 had been recorded as commission income.
questions.text: Sales for $120 had been recorded as commission income.

questions.question: A computer repair for $95 had been recorded as computer equipment. What is Kate’s revised profit for the yearaftercorrecting the errors (ignore depreciation)? $9895D$9825C$9705B$9585A Which document is issued at regular intervals to show the transactions that have taken place between a business and its bank? 11 bank reconciliation statementA bank statementB statement of accountC statement of affairsD 11_7707_13_FP_2025 4 A standing order paid for rent hasnotbeen entered into the accounting records of a business. Which statement is correct? 12 Because the payment was made automatically, no additional entries are required in the accounting records. A Because the payment was made automatically, only an entry in the rent account is required.B Entries are required in the cash book and in the bank reconciliation statement.C Entries are required in the cash book and in the nominal (general) ledger.D Which set of books of prime entry may be used as a source of information for preparing a sales ledger control account? 13 cash book, sales journal, general journalA cash book, sales returns journal, purchases journal, sales journalB purchases journal, general journal, petty cash bookC purchases returns journal, sales returns journal, cash book, sales journalD What is included in a purchases ledger control account?14 cash purchasesA debts written off as irrecoverableB discount allowedC goods returned to credit suppliersD An owner bought machinery for her business costing $5000. This was entered as revenue expenditure in the accounting records. She also invested $2000 of additional capital into the business and entered this as a revenue receipt in the accounting records. What was the net effect of these errors on her profit for the year (ignore depreciation)? 15 $3000 overstatedA $3000 understatedB $7000 overstatedC $7000 understatedD 11_7707_13_FP_2025 5, A motor vehicle was bought by T Limited for $30000 on 1 January 2022. It was depreciated at the rate of 30% per annum using the reducing balance method. The financial year end of T Limited is

questions.question: December. What is the net book value of the motor vehicle at 31 December 2024? 16 $14700D$12000C$10290B$3000A Susan is a trader. Her financial year end is 5 April. She bought a motor vehicle on 6 April 2023 for $10 000. It was depreciated at 25% on the straight-line basis and was sold on 15 May 2025 for $3000. No depreciation was charged in the year of disposal. What was the profit or loss on disposal? 17 loss $2000A loss $2625B profit $500C profit $3000D At the start of his first year of trading, Jimmy paid $9600 for insurance for 20 months. How did the balancecarried downappear in his insurance ledger account at the end of the first year of trading? 18 $3840 on the credit sideA $3840 on the debit sideB $5760 on the credit sideC $5760 on the debit sideD Which statement about a provision for doubtful debts is correct?19 It ensures that the amount of trade receivables at the year end is realistic.A It is an application of the going concern principle.B The level of the provision must be increased when sales are falling.C The profit for the year increases when the provision for doubtful debts is increased.D 11_7707_13_FP_2025 6 A company provided the following information about its closing inventory.20 $35 per unit (including carriage inwards of $2 per unit) cost price $48 per unit net realisable value Which unit value should the company use to value its closing inventory? $33 per unitA $35 per unitB $37 per unitC $48 per unitD Tim wanted to set up a new business. Why did he prefer to set up as a sole trader rather than as a limited company? 21 1   to be able to make drawings when he wanted 2   to have easier access to bank loans 3   to safeguard his personal assets from suppliers 3 onlyD2 and 3C1 onlyB1 and 2A Why is it important for a sole trader to prepare a statement of financial position for the business?22 to know the net worth of the business at a certain dateA to list all revenues and expenses of the businessB to measure the profitability of business salesC to record all cash receipts and paymentsD 23 X and Y have set up a partnership sharing profits in the ratio 2 : 1. The profit for the first year of trading was $33 000. X is entitled to interest on capital of $2000, and Y receives a partner’s salary of $4000. Interest on drawings charged is $200 for X and $100 for Y. What is X’ s share of the residual profit for the year?, A $17 800 B $18 000 C $18 200 D $21 000 11_7707_13_FP_2025 7 What is the correct order of preparing accounts for a partnership?24 appropriation account→sales account→current accounts A appropriation account→current accounts→sales account B sales account→appropriation account→current accounts C current accounts→appropriation account→sales account D, A limited company raised funds from an issue of debentures. Which statement is correct? 25 The debentures are part of the equity of the company.A The debenture holders cannot vote at the annual general meeting.B The debenture holders are repaid only if the company is wound up.C The debenture interest is paid only if the company earns a profit.D What isnotincluded in a statement of changes in equity of a limited company for the year ended 31 March 2025? 26 a proposed final dividend which is to be paid to ordinary shareholders on 1 August 2025A a transfer made to the general reserve from retained earnings on 31 March 2025 B an interim dividend paid to the ordinary shareholders on 1 November 2024C an issue of additional ordinary shares made on 31 October 2024D 11_7707_13_FP_2025 8, A club’s income and expenditure for the year was as follows:27 $$ income: 1260subscriptions expenditure: 950general expenses 1090140depreciation of equipment 170 What does the figure of$170 represent? deficit for the yearA loss for the yearB profit for the yearC surplus for the yearD The following amounts relate to a manufacturing business for the year ended 30 September 2025.28 $item 355000 prime cost 1 14 850 factory overheads 475000 total production cost of finished goods 4200 inventory of raw materials at 30 September 2025 1 100 inventory of finished products at 30 September 2025 Work in progress at 30 September 2024 was $9990. What was the total inventory of the business at 30 September 2025? $10140D$5300C$5150B$4840A 11_7707_13_FP_2025 9, A manufacturing account and income statement are being prepared for a business that makes chairs and desks for schools. The business pays wages to the following employees. 29 1    the carpenters who make the chairs and desks 2    the cleaner who cleans the offices of the accounts and sales staff 3    the lorry driver who delivers the finished chairs and desks to schools 4    the supervisor in charge of the carpenters Which wages will be included as an expense in the income statement? 2 and 4D2 and 3C1 and 4B1 and 2A, A business using incomplete records provided data from its statement of affairs. During the year, the owner invested $6000 from his private funds and withdrew $3250 of the profit for the year for personal use. 30 closing statement of affairs $ opening statement of affairs $ 5200040000assets 800015000liabilities How much was the profit for the year? $62500D$38750C$31250B$16250A 11_7707_13_FP_2025 10 Joseph doesnotmaintain afull set of accounting records. He is able to supply the amount that is owed by his trade receivables at the start of the accounting year. What other information is required to calculate Joseph’s credit sales for the year? 31 The total amount received from credit customers.1 The total amount of trade discount allowed to credit customers.2 The total sales value of goods sold on credit that were returned.3 The amount that remains due from credit customers at the end of the accounting year.4 1, 2, 3 and 4A 1 and 2 onlyB 1, 3 and 4 onlyC 4 onlyD Senga is concerned that the current ratio of her business is worsening each year. She has suggested the following measures. 32 1   Increase cash purchases of inventory. 2   Introduce further capital in the form of cash. 3   Decrease the rate of depreciation on non-current assets. 4   Sell off surplus non-current assets. Which two measures would improve the current ratio of the business? 2 and 4D2 and 3C1 and 4B1 and 3A 11_7707_13_FP_2025 11 Ali owns a clothing shop. He is comparing his accounting ratios with the ratios of Hajar who owns a similar clothing shop. The following information is available. 33 HajarAli 20%45%gross margin 20 times15 timesrate of inventory turnover What would explain these accounting ratios? 1   Ali controlled his cost of sales better than Hajar. 2   Ali controlled his expenses better than Hajar. 3   Ali sold goods at lower prices than Hajar. 4   Ali sold his goods more slowly than Hajar. 3 and 4 onlyD2, 3 and 4C1 and 4B1, 2 and 3A Which action is an application of the materiality principle?34 always using the straight-line method when depreciating assetsA buying raw materials on credit rather than paying immediatelyB charging the cost of light bulbs and batteries to general expensesC recording goods sold only when their legal title passes to the buyerD Which factor relating to the quality of financial information requires that such information is free from significant errors and bias? 35 comparabilityA consistencyB reliabilityC understandabilityD Permission  to  reproduce  items  where  third-party  owned  material  protected  by  copyright  is  included  has  been  sought  and  cleared  where  possible. A manufacturing account and income statement are being prepared for a business that makes chairs and desks for schools. The business pays wages to the following employees. 29 1    the carpenters who make the chairs and desks 2    the cleaner who cleans the offices of the accounts and sales staff 3    the lorry driver who delivers the finished chairs and desks to schools 4    the supervisor in charge of the carpenters Which wages will be included as an expense in the income statement? 2 and 4D2 and 3C1 and 4B1 and 2A
